revision.text: == Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors == {{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021}} The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10}}{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018}} --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[ja:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Characteristics |- ! colspan=5 | Tohoku Region |- | {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|会津大塚山古墳}}|| Fukushima Prefecture, {{ill|Aizuwakamatsu|ja|会津若松市}} || || 4th century || Among the oldest in Tohoku, designated as a National Historic Site in 1972. |- ! colspan=5 | Kanto Region |- | {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Burial Mound|ja|前橋天神山古墳}} || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}} || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 4th century || Two Four-God Four-Beast Mirrors excavated. |- | {{ill|Tamonoko Burial Mound|ja|頼母子古墳}} (destroyed) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|ja|太田市}} || Unknown (Keyhole-shaped mound is likely) || Mid-4th century || Two triangular-rimmed mirrors (one location unknown). |- | {{ill|Kitayama Chausuyama Burial Mound|ja|北山茶臼山古墳}} || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Tomioka|ja|富岡市}} || Round mound (possible keyhole shape) || Late 4th century || |- | {{ill|Kanizawa Burial Mound|ja|蟹沢古墳}} (destroyed) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}} || Round mound (estimated, possibly square mound) || || Mirror with "Zhengshi" (正始) inscription. Same mold as former Motoda No. 48 mound. |- | {{ill|Akagitsuka Burial Mound|ja|赤城塚古墳}} (Nishioka Shrine Burial Mound) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}} || Round mound || 4th century || Triangular-rimmed Buddha Beast Mirror excavated. |- | Three burial mounds in {{ill|Sanbongi|ja|三本木}} || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}} || Unknown || 4th century || Three triangular-rimmed mirrors excavated. Likely the {{ill|Kanda Asama Shrine Burial Mound|ja|神田浅間神社古墳}}. |- | {{ill|Tekozuka Burial Mound|ja|手古塚古墳}} (destroyed) || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}} || || || Same mold as Kawabe Same mold as Maebashi Tenjinyama Mound<ref name="miyazaki" />. Extensively looted in the early Shōwa period<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1849|title=Cultural Properties of Miyazaki: Mochida Burial Mounds |accessdate=2018-10-23}}</ref>. |} [[category:Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror forks]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Important cultural properties of Japan]] [[ja:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]]

== Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors == {{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021}} The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10}}{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018}} --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[ja:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Same mold as former Motoda No. 48 mound. |- | {{ill|Akagitsuka Burial Mound|ja|赤城塚古墳}} (Nishioka Shrine Burial Mound) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}} || Round mound || 4th century || Triangular-rimmed Buddha Beast Mirror excavated. |- | Three burial mounds in {{ill|Sanbongi|ja|三本木}} || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}} || Unknown || 4th century || Three triangular-rimmed mirrors excavated. Likely the {{ill|Kanda Asama Shrine Burial Mound|ja|神田浅間神社古墳}}. |- | {{ill|Tekozuka Burial Mound|ja|手古塚古墳}} (destroyed) || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}} || || || Same mold as Kawabe Same mold as Maebashi Tenjinyama Mound<ref name="miyazaki" />. Extensively looted in the early Shōwa period<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1849|title=Cultural Properties of Miyazaki: Mochida Burial Mounds |accessdate=2018-10-23}}</ref>. |} [[category:Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror forks]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Important cultural properties of Japan]] [[ja:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]]

revision.text:
header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021}} The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10}}{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018}} --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[ja:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Characteristics |- ! colspan=5 | Tohoku Region |- | {{ill|Kanizawa Burial Mound|ja|蟹沢古墳}} (destroyed) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Takasaki|ja|高崎市}} || Round mound (estimated, possibly square mound) || || Mirror with "Zhengshi" (正始) inscription. Same mold as former Motoda No. 48 mound. |- | {{ill|Akagitsuka Burial Mound|ja|赤城塚古墳}} (Nishioka Shrine Burial Mound) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Itakura|ja|板倉町}} || Round mound || 4th century || Triangular-rimmed Buddha Beast Mirror excavated. |- | Three burial mounds in {{ill|Sanbongi|ja|三本木}} || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|ja|藤岡市}} || Unknown || 4th century || Three triangular-rimmed mirrors excavated. Likely the {{ill|Kanda Asama Shrine Burial Mound|ja|神田浅間神社古墳}}. |- | {{ill|Tekozuka Burial Mound|ja|手古塚古墳}} (destroyed) || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Kisarazu|ja|木更津市}} || || || Same mold as Kawabe Burial Mound, Shimane Prefecture. |- | {{ill|Shiroyama No. 1 Burial Mound (Katori)|ja|城山1号墳 (香取市)}} || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Komiyama Town || Keyhole-shaped mound || Mid-6th century || Mirrors excavated from a later burial mound. |- | {{ill|Matoda Ōtsukayama Burial Mound|ja|真土大塚山古墳}} (destroyed) || Kanagawa Prefecture, {{ill|Hiratsuka, Kanagawa|ja|平塚市}}, Nishimatoda || || header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[ja:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Fukuoka Prefecture, {{ill|Munakata, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:宗像市]], Okinoshima || || Late 4th to early 5th century || Ancient ritual site, not a burial mound |- | {{ill|Akasuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:赤塚古墳]] || mound; five mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Mochida Burial Mounds|en}}[[ja:持田古墳群]] || Miyazaki Prefecture, {{ill|Koyu District, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:児湯郡]], {{ill|Takanabe, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:高鍋町]], Mochida || || || Mirror inscribed "Heavenly King Sun and Moon." header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[ja:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Characteristics |- ! colspan=5 | Tohoku Region |- | {{ill|Kanizawa Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:蟹沢古墳]] (destroyed) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Takasaki|en}}[[ja:高崎市]] || Round mound (estimated, possibly square mound) || || Mirror with "Zhengshi" (正始) inscription. Same mold as former Motoda No. 48 mound. |- | {{ill|Akagitsuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:赤城塚古墳]] (Nishioka Shrine Burial Mound) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Itakura|en}}[[ja:板倉町]] || Round mound || 4th century || Triangular-rimmed Buddha Beast Mirror excavated. |- | Three burial mounds in {{ill|Sanbongi|en}}[[ja:三本木]] || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en}}[[ja:藤岡市]] || Unknown || 4th century || Three triangular-rimmed mirrors excavated. Likely the {{ill|Kanda Asama Shrine Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:神田浅間神社古墳]]. |- | {{ill|Tekozuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:手古塚古墳]] (destroyed) || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Kisarazu|en}}[[ja:木更津市]] || || || Same mold as Kawabe Burial Mound, Shimane Prefecture. |- | {{ill|Shiroyama No. 1 Burial Mound (Katori)|en}}[[ja:城山1号墳 (香取市)]] || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en}}[[ja:香取市]], Komiyama Town || Keyhole-shaped mound || Mid-6th century || Mirrors excavated from a later burial mound. |- | {{ill|Matoda Ōtsukayama Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:真土大塚山古墳]] (destroyed) || Kanagawa Prefecture, Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 4th century || |- ! colspan=5 | Chubu Region |- | {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:甲斐銚子塚古墳]] || Yamanashi Prefecture, {{ill|Kōfu|en}}[[ja:甲府市]], Shimosone Town || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 4th century || |- | {{ill|Daimaruyama Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:大丸山古墳]] || Yamanashi Prefecture, Kōfu, Shimosone Town || Keyhole-shaped mound || Mid-4th century || |- | Prefecture, {{ill|Hamamatsu|en}}[[ja:浜松市]] || Keyhole-shaped mound || Early 4th century || Same mold as Tsubai Ōtsukayama Mound, Sameda Treasure Mound. |- | {{ill|Shinpōinzan Burial header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Characteristics |- ! colspan=5 | Tohoku Region |- | {{ill|Kanizawa Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:蟹沢古墳]] (destroyed) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Takasaki|en}}[[ja:高崎市]] || Round mound (estimated, possibly square mound) || || Mirror with "Zhengshi" (正始) inscription. Same mold as former Motoda No. 48 mound. |- | {{ill|Akagitsuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:赤城塚古墳]] (Nishioka Shrine Burial Mound) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Itakura|en}}[[ja:板倉町]] || Round mound || 4th century || Triangular-rimmed Buddha Beast Mirror excavated. |- | Three burial mounds in {{ill|Sanbongi|en}}[[ja:三本木]] || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en}}[[ja:藤岡市]] || Unknown || 4th century || Three triangular-rimmed mirrors excavated. Likely the {{ill|Kanda Asama Shrine Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:神田浅間神社古墳]]. |- | {{ill|Tekozuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:手古塚古墳]] (destroyed) || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Kisarazu|en}}[[ja:木更津市]] || || || Same revision.comment: getting rid of this one that is blocking a lot of pages
revision.text:
header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Characteristics |- ! colspan=5 | Tohoku Region |- | {{ill|Kanizawa Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:蟹沢古墳]] (destroyed) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Takasaki|en}}[[ja:高崎市]] || Round mound (estimated, possibly square mound) || || Mirror with "Zhengshi" (正始) inscription. Same mold as former Motoda No. 48 mound. |- | {{ill|Akagitsuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:赤城塚古墳]] (Nishioka Shrine Burial Mound) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Itakura|en}}[[ja:板倉町]] || Round mound || 4th century || Triangular-rimmed Buddha Beast Mirror excavated. |- | Three burial mounds in {{ill|Sanbongi|en}}[[ja:三本木]] || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en}}[[ja:藤岡市]] || Unknown || 4th century || Three triangular-rimmed mirrors excavated. Likely the {{ill|Kanda Asama Shrine Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:神田浅間神社古墳]]. |- | {{ill|Tekozuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:手古塚古墳]] (destroyed) || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Kisarazu|en}}[[ja:木更津市]] || || || Same header Significant Kofun with Triangular-Rimmed Mirrors header
{{Miscellaneous content bullet list|section=1|date=April 2021]] The distribution of triangular-rimmed mirrors with deities and beasts by prefecture {{citation needed|date=2018-10]]{{questionable source range|1=shows that the highest number is found in Nara Prefecture with 100 mirrors, followed by Kyoto Prefecture with 66, Hyōgo Prefecture with over 40, and Osaka Prefecture with 38. The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) For example, over 40 mirrors have been found in Fukuoka Prefecture and over 28 in the Kibi region. This suggests that the Kinki region (Kyoto, Nara, Osaka) centered on Nara accounts for a significant number of finds. Furthermore, the number of excavations in the Kinki region exceeds 270, making up more than half of the total. --><!--|title=Sources on total number of mirrors and their distribution are unclear. See notes for details.|talksection=About the number of mirrors and their distribution|date=October 2018]] --> [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Characteristics |- ! colspan=5 | Tohoku Region |- | {{ill|Kanizawa Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:蟹沢古墳]] (destroyed) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Takasaki|en}}[[ja:高崎市]] || Round mound (estimated, possibly square mound) || || Mirror with "Zhengshi" (正始) inscription. Same mold as former Motoda No. 48 mound. |- | {{ill|Akagitsuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:赤城塚古墳]] (Nishioka Shrine Burial Mound) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Itakura|en}}[[ja:板倉町]] || Round mound || 4th century || Triangular-rimmed Buddha Beast Mirror excavated. |- | Three burial mounds in {{ill|Sanbongi|en}}[[ja:三本木]] || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en}}[[ja:藤岡市]] || Unknown || 4th century || Three triangular-rimmed mirrors excavated. Likely the {{ill|Kanda Asama Shrine Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:神田浅間神社古墳]]. |- | {{ill|Tekozuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:手古塚古墳]] (destroyed) || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Kisarazu|en}}[[ja:木更津市]] || || || Same mold as Kawabe Burial Mound, Shimane Prefecture. |- | {{ill|Shiroyama No. 1 Burial Mound (Katori)|en}}[[ja:城山1号墳 (香取市)]] || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en}}[[ja:香取市]], Komiyama Town || Keyhole-shaped mound || Mid-6th century || Mirrors excavated from a later burial mound. |- | {{ill|Matoda Ōtsukayama Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:真土大塚山古墳]] (destroyed) || Kanagawa Prefecture, {{ill|Hiratsuka, Kanagawa|en}}[[ja:平塚市]], Nishimatoda || || || |- | {{ill|Hakusan Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:白山古墳]] (destroyed) || Kanagawa Prefecture, {{ill|Kawasaki City|en}}[[ja:川崎市]], Saiwai Ward || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 4th century || |- ! colspan=5 | Chubu Region |- | {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:甲斐銚子塚古墳]] || Yamanashi Prefecture, {{ill|Kōfu|en}}[[ja:甲府市]], Shimosone Town || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 4th century || |- | {{ill|Daimaruyama Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:大丸山古墳]] || Yamanashi Prefecture, Kōfu, Shimosone Town || Keyhole-shaped mound || Mid-4th century || |- | {{ill|Hanayano Burial Mound No. 1|en}}[[ja:花野谷1号墳]] || Fukui Prefecture, {{ill|Fukui|en}}[[ja:福井市]] || Round mound || || Discovered in September 2000. |- | {{ill|Otanaka Crown Prince Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:小田中親王塚古墳]] || Ishikawa Prefecture, {{ill|Noto District, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:鹿島郡]] || Round mound || Late 4th century to end || |- | {{ill|Mori General's Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:森将軍塚古墳]] || Prefecture, {{ill|Hamamatsu|en}}[[ja:浜松市]] || Keyhole-shaped mound || Early 4th century || Same mold as Tsubai Ōtsukayama Mound, Sameda Treasure Mound. |- | {{ill|Shinpōinzan Burial Shizuoka|en}}[[ja:菊川市]] || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 4th century || One mirror inscribed "Heavenly King Sun and Moon," along with two other mirrors |- | {{ill|Gōōdōyama Burial Mound No. mirrors excavated |- | {{ill|Kabutoyama Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:兜山古墳]] || Aichi Prefecture, {{ill|Tōkai, Aichi|en}}[[ja:東海市]] || Round mound || || Three-God Three-Beast Mirror |- | {{ill|Hakusanyabu The mirrors are predominantly excavated in the Kinki region, centered on the former [[Yamato Province]], but they are distributed nationwide from Kyushu to Tohoku. <!-- (Due to the lack of valid sources for the number of excavations in Fukuoka Prefecture, this part has been omitted temporarily.) '''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following burial mounds (kofun) in Japan:
* **{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}** – A kofun located in **Nakano, Nagano Prefecture**. It is a **designated historic site of Nakano City**. * **{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}** – A kofun formerly located in **Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture**. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a **mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)**, are **designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan**.

'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following burial mounds (kofun) in Japan:
* **{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}** – A kofun located in **Nakano, Nagano Prefecture**. It is a **designated historic site of Nakano City**. * **{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}** – A kofun formerly located in **Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture**. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a **mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)**, are **designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan**.

'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following burial mounds (kofun) in Japan:
* **{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}** – A kofun located in **Nakano, Nagano Prefecture**. It is a **designated historic site of Nakano City**. * **{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}** – A kofun formerly located in **Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture**. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a **mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)**, are **designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan**.

revision.text:
'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following burial mounds (kofun) in Japan:
* **{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}** – A kofun located in **Nakano, Nagano Prefecture**. It is a **designated historic site of Nakano City**. * **{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}** – A kofun formerly located in **Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture**. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a **mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)**, are **designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan**.
{{Disambiguation}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanizawa Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Characteristics |- ! colspan=5 | Tohoku Region |- | {{ill|Kanizawa Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:蟹沢古墳]] (destroyed) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Takasaki|en}}[[ja:高崎市]] || Round mound (estimated, possibly square mound) || || Mirror with "Zhengshi" (正始) inscription. Same mold as former Motoda No. 48 mound. |- | {{ill|Akagitsuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:赤城塚古墳]] (Nishioka Shrine Burial Mound) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Itakura|en}}[[ja:板倉町]] || Round mound || 4th century || Triangular-rimmed Buddha Beast Mirror excavated. |- | Three burial mounds in {{ill|Sanbongi|en}}[[ja:三本木]] || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en}}[[ja:藤岡市]] || Unknown || 4th century || Three triangular-rimmed mirrors excavated. Likely the {{ill|Kanda Asama Shrine Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:神田浅間神社古墳]]. |- | {{ill|Tekozuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:手古塚古墳]] (destroyed) || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Kisarazu|en}}[[ja:木更津市]] || || || Same mold as Kawabe Burial Mound, Shimane Prefecture. |- | {{ill|Shiroyama No. 1 Burial Mound (Katori)|en}}[[ja:城山1号墳 (香取市)]] || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en}}[[ja:香取市]], Komiyama Town || Keyhole-shaped mound || Mid-6th century || Mirrors excavated from a later burial mound. |- | {{ill|Matoda Ōtsukayama Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:真土大塚山古墳]] (destroyed) || Kanagawa Prefecture, {{ill|Hiratsuka, Kanagawa|en}}[[ja:平塚市]], Nishimatoda || || || |- | {{ill|Hakusan Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:白山古墳]] (destroyed) || Kanagawa Prefecture, {{ill|Kawasaki City|en}}[[ja:川崎市]], Saiwai Ward || || Keyhole-shaped mound || Late 4th century || |- | {{ill|Daimaruyama Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:大丸山古墳]] || Yamanashi Prefecture, Kōfu, Shimosone Town || Keyhole-shaped mound || Mid-4th century || |- | Mound|en}}[[ja:小田中親王塚古墳]] || Ishikawa Prefecture, {{ill|Noto District, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:鹿島郡]] || Round mound || Late 4th century to end || |- | {{ill|Mori General's Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:森将軍塚古墳]] || revision.text:
'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following burial mounds (kofun) in Japan:
* **{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}** – A kofun located in **Nakano, Nagano Prefecture**. It is a **designated historic site of Nakano City**. * **{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}** – A kofun formerly located in **Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture**. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a **mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)**, are **designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan**.
{{Disambiguation}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanizawa Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Japanese burial mounds]] to [[Category:kofun]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following burial mounds (kofun) in Japan:
* **{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}** – A kofun located in **Nakano, Nagano Prefecture**. It is a **designated historic site of Nakano City**. * **{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}** – A kofun formerly located in **Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture**. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a **mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)**, are **designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan**.
{{Disambiguation}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanizawa Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:kofun]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Characteristics |- ! colspan=5 | Tohoku Region |- | {{ill|Kanizawa Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:蟹沢古墳]] (destroyed) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Takasaki|en}}[[ja:高崎市]] || Round mound (estimated, possibly square mound) || || Mirror with "Zhengshi" (正始) inscription. Same mold as former Motoda No. 48 mound. |- | {{ill|Akagitsuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:赤城塚古墳]] (Nishioka Shrine Burial Mound) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Itakura|en}}[[ja:板倉町]] || Round mound || 4th century || Triangular-rimmed Buddha Beast Mirror excavated. |- | Three burial mounds in {{ill|Sanbongi|en}}[[ja:三本木]] || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en}}[[ja:藤岡市]] || Unknown || 4th century || Three triangular-rimmed mirrors excavated. Likely the {{ill|Kanda Asama Shrine Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:神田浅間神社古墳]]. |- | {{ill|Tekozuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:手古塚古墳]] (destroyed) || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Kisarazu|en}}[[ja:木更津市]] || || || Same mold as Kawabe Burial Mound, Shimane Prefecture. |- | {{ill|Shiroyama No. 1 Burial Mound (Katori)|en}}[[ja:城山1号墳 (香取市)]] || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en}}[[ja:香取市]], Komiyama Town || Keyhole-shaped mound || Mid-6th century || Mirrors excavated from a later burial mound. |- | {{ill|Matoda Ōtsukayama Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:真土大塚山古墳]] (destroyed) || Kanagawa Prefecture, {{ill|Hiratsuka, Kanagawa|en}}[[ja:平塚市]], Nishimatoda || || || |- | {{ill|Hakusan Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:白山古墳]] (destroyed) || Kanagawa Prefecture, {{ill|Kawasaki City|en}}[[ja:川崎市]], Saiwai Ward || revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:New pages]] to [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following burial mounds (kofun) in Japan:
* **{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}** – A kofun located in **Nakano, Nagano Prefecture**. It is a **designated historic site of Nakano City**. * **{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}** – A kofun formerly located in **Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture**. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a **mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)**, are **designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan**.
{{Disambiguation}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanizawa Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:kofun]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Characteristics |- ! colspan=5 | Tohoku Region |- | {{ill|Kanizawa Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:蟹沢古墳]] (destroyed) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Takasaki|en}}[[ja:高崎市]] || Round mound (estimated, possibly square mound) || || Mirror with "Zhengshi" (正始) inscription. Same mold as former Motoda No. 48 mound. |- | {{ill|Akagitsuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:赤城塚古墳]] (Nishioka Shrine Burial Mound) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Itakura|en}}[[ja:板倉町]] || Round mound || 4th century || Triangular-rimmed Buddha Beast Mirror excavated. |- | Three burial mounds in {{ill|Sanbongi|en}}[[ja:三本木]] || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en}}[[ja:藤岡市]] || Unknown || 4th century || Three triangular-rimmed mirrors excavated. Likely the {{ill|Kanda Asama Shrine Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:神田浅間神社古墳]]. |- | {{ill|Tekozuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:手古塚古墳]] (destroyed) || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Kisarazu|en}}[[ja:木更津市]] || || || Same revision.text:
'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following burial mounds (kofun) in Japan:
* **{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}** – A kofun located in **Nakano, Nagano Prefecture**. It is a **designated historic site of Nakano City**. * **{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}** – A kofun formerly located in **Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture**. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a **mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)**, are **designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan**.
{{Disambiguation}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanizawa Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:kofun]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Characteristics |- ! colspan=5 | Tohoku Region |- | {{ill|Kanizawa Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:蟹沢古墳]] (destroyed) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Takasaki|en}}[[ja:高崎市]] || Round mound (estimated, possibly square mound) || || Mirror with "Zhengshi" (正始) inscription. Same mold as former Motoda No. 48 mound. |- | {{ill|Akagitsuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:赤城塚古墳]] (Nishioka Shrine Burial Mound) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Itakura|en}}[[ja:板倉町]] || Round mound || 4th century || Triangular-rimmed Buddha Beast Mirror excavated. |- | Three burial mounds in {{ill|Sanbongi|en}}[[ja:三本木]] || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en}}[[ja:藤岡市]] || Unknown || 4th century || Three triangular-rimmed mirrors excavated. Likely the {{ill|Kanda Asama Shrine Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:神田浅間神社古墳]]. |- | {{ill|Tekozuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:手古塚古墳]] (destroyed) || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Kisarazu|en}}[[ja:木更津市]] || || || Same mold as Kawabe Burial Mound, Shimane Prefecture. |- | {{ill|Shiroyama No. 1 Burial Mound (Katori)|en}}[[ja:城山1号墳 (香取市)]] || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en}}[[ja:香取市]], Komiyama Town || Keyhole-shaped mound || Mid-6th century || Mirrors excavated from a later burial mound. |- | {{ill|Matoda Ōtsukayama Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:真土大塚山古墳]] (destroyed) || Kanagawa Prefecture, revision.text:
'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following burial mounds (kofun) in Japan:
* **{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}** – A kofun located in **Nakano, Nagano Prefecture**. It is a **designated historic site of Nakano City**. * **{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}** – A kofun formerly located in **Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture**. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a **mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)**, are **designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan**.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:null edit to force updates]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following burial mounds (kofun) in Japan:
* **{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}** – A kofun located in **Nakano, Nagano Prefecture**. It is a **designated historic site of Nakano City**. * **{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}** – A kofun formerly located in **Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture**. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a **mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)**, are **designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan**.
{{Disambiguation}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanizawa Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:kofun]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Characteristics |- ! colspan=5 | Tohoku Region |- | {{ill|Kanizawa Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:蟹沢古墳]] (destroyed) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Takasaki|en}}[[ja:高崎市]] || Round mound (estimated, possibly square mound) || || Mirror with "Zhengshi" (正始) inscription. Same mold as former Motoda No. 48 mound. |- | {{ill|Akagitsuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:赤城塚古墳]] (Nishioka Shrine Burial Mound) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Itakura|en}}[[ja:板倉町]] || Round mound || 4th century || Triangular-rimmed Buddha Beast Mirror excavated. |- | Three burial mounds in {{ill|Sanbongi|en}}[[ja:三本木]] || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en}}[[ja:藤岡市]] || Unknown || 4th century || Three triangular-rimmed mirrors excavated. Likely the {{ill|Kanda Asama Shrine Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:神田浅間神社古墳]]. |- | {{ill|Tekozuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:手古塚古墳]] (destroyed) || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Kisarazu|en}}[[ja:木更津市]] || || || Same mold as Kawabe Burial Mound, Shimane Prefecture. |- | {{ill|Shiroyama No. 1 Burial Mound (Katori)|en}}[[ja:城山1号墳 (香取市)]] || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en}}[[ja:香取市]], Komiyama Town || {{ill|Hiratsuka, Kanagawa|en}}[[ja:平塚市]], Nishimatoda || || || |- | {{ill|Hakusan Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:白山古墳]] (destroyed) || Kanagawa Prefecture, {{ill|Kawasaki City|en}}[[ja:川崎市]], Saiwai Ward || '''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following burial mounds (kofun) in Japan:
* **{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}** – A kofun located in **Nakano, Nagano Prefecture**. It is a **designated historic site of Nakano City**. * **{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}** – A kofun formerly located in **Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture**. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a **mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)**, are **designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan**.

'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following burial mounds (kofun) in Japan:
* **{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}** – A kofun located in **Nakano, Nagano Prefecture**. It is a **designated historic site of Nakano City**. * **{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}** – A kofun formerly located in **Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture**. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a **mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)**, are **designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan**.

'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following burial mounds (kofun) in Japan:
* **{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}** – A kofun located in **Nakano, Nagano Prefecture**. It is a **designated historic site of Nakano City**. * **{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}** – A kofun formerly located in **Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture**. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a **mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)**, are **designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan**.

'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following burial mounds (kofun) in Japan:
* **{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}** – A kofun located in **Nakano, Nagano Prefecture**. It is a **designated historic site of Nakano City**. * **{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}** – A kofun formerly located in **Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture**. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a **mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)**, are **designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan**.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Individual Kofun]] to [[Category:kk]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following burial mounds (kofun) in Japan:
* **{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}** – A kofun located in **Nakano, Nagano Prefecture**. It is a **designated historic site of Nakano City**. * **{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}** – A kofun formerly located in **Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture**. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a **mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)**, are **designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan**.
{{Disambiguation}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanizawa Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Characteristics |- ! colspan=5 | Tohoku Region |- | {{ill|Kanizawa Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:蟹沢古墳]] (destroyed) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Takasaki|en}}[[ja:高崎市]] || Round mound (estimated, possibly square mound) || || Mirror with "Zhengshi" (正始) inscription. Same mold as former Motoda No. 48 mound. |- | {{ill|Akagitsuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:赤城塚古墳]] (Nishioka Shrine Burial Mound) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Itakura|en}}[[ja:板倉町]] || Round mound || 4th century || Triangular-rimmed Buddha Beast Mirror excavated. |- | Three burial mounds in {{ill|Sanbongi|en}}[[ja:三本木]] || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en}}[[ja:藤岡市]] || Unknown || 4th century || Three triangular-rimmed mirrors excavated. Likely the {{ill|Kanda Asama Shrine Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:神田浅間神社古墳]]. |- | {{ill|Tekozuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:手古塚古墳]] (destroyed) || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Kisarazu|en}}[[ja:木更津市]] || || || Same mold as Kawabe Burial Mound, Shimane Prefecture. |- | {{ill|Shiroyama No. 1 Burial Mound (Katori)|en}}[[ja:城山1号墳 (香取市)]] || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en}}[[ja:香取市]], Komiyama Town || {{ill|Hiratsuka, Kanagawa|en}}[[ja:平塚市]], Nishimatoda || || || |- | {{ill|Hakusan Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:白山古墳]] (destroyed) || Kanagawa Prefecture, {{ill|Kawasaki City|en}}[[ja:川崎市]], Saiwai Ward || revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Individual Kofun]] to [[Category:kkkk]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following burial mounds (kofun) in Japan:
* **{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}** – A kofun located in **Nakano, Nagano Prefecture**. It is a **designated historic site of Nakano City**. * **{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}** – A kofun formerly located in **Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture**. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a **mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)**, are **designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan**.
{{Disambiguation}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kanizawa Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:Individual Kofun]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Historical mirrors in Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:smaller; width:100%;" |+ Detailed Distribution |- ! Name of Burial Mound !! Location !! Shape !! Period !! Characteristics |- ! colspan=5 | Tohoku Region |- | {{ill|Kanizawa Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:蟹沢古墳]] (destroyed) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Takasaki|en}}[[ja:高崎市]] || Round mound (estimated, possibly square mound) || || Mirror with "Zhengshi" (正始) inscription. Same mold as former Motoda No. 48 mound. |- | {{ill|Akagitsuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:赤城塚古墳]] (Nishioka Shrine Burial Mound) || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Itakura|en}}[[ja:板倉町]] || Round mound || 4th century || Triangular-rimmed Buddha Beast Mirror excavated. |- | Three burial mounds in {{ill|Sanbongi|en}}[[ja:三本木]] || Gunma Prefecture, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en}}[[ja:藤岡市]] || Unknown || 4th century || Three triangular-rimmed mirrors excavated. Likely the {{ill|Kanda Asama Shrine Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:神田浅間神社古墳]]. |- | {{ill|Tekozuka Burial Mound|en}}[[ja:手古塚古墳]] (destroyed) || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Kisarazu|en}}[[ja:木更津市]] || || || Same mold as Kawabe Burial Mound, Shimane Prefecture. |- | {{ill|Shiroyama No. 1 Burial Mound (Katori)|en}}[[ja:城山1号墳 (香取市)]] || Chiba Prefecture, {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en}}[[ja:香取市]], Komiyama Town || revision.text:
'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following burial mounds (kofun) in Japan:
* **{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}** – A kofun located in **Nakano, Nagano Prefecture**. It is a **designated historic site of Nakano City**. * **{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}** – A kofun formerly located in **Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture**. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a **mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)**, are **designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan**.

'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following burial mounds (kofun) in Japan:
* **{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}** – A kofun located in **Nakano, Nagano Prefecture**. It is a **designated historic site of Nakano City**. * **{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}** – A kofun formerly located in **Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture**. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a **mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)**, are **designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan**.

* **{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}** – A kofun located in **Nakano, Nagano Prefecture**. It is a **designated historic site of Nakano City**. * **{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}** – A kofun formerly located in **Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture**. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a **mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)**, are **designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan**.

'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following burial mounds (kofun) in Japan:
* **{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}** – A kofun located in **Nakano, Nagano Prefecture**. It is a **designated historic site of Nakano City**. * **{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}** – A kofun formerly located in **Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture**. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a **mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)**, are **designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan**.

'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following burial mounds (kofun) in Japan:
* **{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}** – A kofun located in **Nakano, Nagano Prefecture**. It is a **designated historic site of Nakano City**. * **{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}** – A kofun formerly located in **Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture**. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a **mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)**, are **designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan**.

It is a **designated historic site of Nakano City**. * **{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}** – A kofun formerly located in **Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture**. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a **mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)**, are **designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan**.

'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following burial mounds (kofun) in Japan:
* **{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}** – A kofun located in **Nakano, Nagano Prefecture**. It is a **designated historic site of Nakano City**. * **{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}** – A kofun formerly located in **Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture**. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a **mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)**, are **designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan**.

It is a **designated historic site of Nakano City**. * **{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}** – A kofun formerly located in **Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture**. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a **mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)**, are **designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan**.

It is a '''designated historic site of Nakano City'''. * '''{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}''' – A kofun formerly located in '''Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture'''. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a '''mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)''', are '''designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan'''.

'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following burial mounds (kofun) in Japan:
* '''{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}''' – A kofun located in '''Nakano, Nagano Prefecture'''. It is a '''designated historic site of Nakano City'''. * '''{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}''' – A kofun formerly located in '''Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture'''. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a '''mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)''', are '''designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan'''.

revision.text:
'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following burial mounds (kofun) in Japan:
* '''{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}''' – A kofun located in '''Nakano, Nagano Prefecture'''. It is a '''designated historic site of Nakano City'''. * '''{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}''' – A kofun formerly located in '''Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture'''. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a '''mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)''', are '''designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan'''.

'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following burial mounds (kofun) in Japan:
* '''{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}''' – A kofun located in '''Nakano, Nagano Prefecture'''. It is a '''designated historic site of Nakano City'''. * '''{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}''' – A kofun formerly located in '''Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture'''. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a '''mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)''', are '''designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan'''.

revision.text:
'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following burial mounds (kofun) in Japan:
* '''{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}''' – A kofun located in '''Nakano, Nagano Prefecture'''. It is a '''designated historic site of Nakano City'''. * '''{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}''' – A kofun formerly located in '''Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture'''. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a '''mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)''', are '''designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan'''.

'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following Kofuns (kofun) in Japan:
* '''{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}''' – A kofun located in '''Nakano, Nagano Prefecture'''. It is a '''designated historic site of Nakano City'''. * '''{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}''' – A kofun formerly located in '''Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture'''. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a '''mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)''', are '''designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan'''.

'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following Kofuns (kofun) in Japan:
* '''{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}''' – A kofun located in '''Nakano, Nagano Prefecture'''. It is a '''designated historic site of Nakano City'''. * '''{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}''' – A kofun formerly located in '''Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture'''. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a '''mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)''', are '''designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan'''.

revision.text:
'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following Kofuns (kofun) in Japan:
* '''{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)}}''' – A kofun located in '''Nakano, Nagano Prefecture'''. It is a '''designated historic site of Nakano City'''. * '''{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}}''' – A kofun formerly located in '''Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture'''. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a '''mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)''', are '''designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan'''.

revision.text:
'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following Kofuns (kofun) in Japan:
* '''{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)|12=draft|12=draft}}''' – A kofun located in '''Nakano, Nagano Prefecture'''. It is a '''designated historic site of Nakano City'''. * '''{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}''' – A kofun formerly located in '''Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture'''. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a '''mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)''', are '''designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan'''.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]] <!--Kanizawa Kofun--> <!--Kanizawa Kofun-->
{{aimai}}

* '''{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' – A kofun located in '''Nakano, Nagano Prefecture'''. It is a '''designated historic site of Nakano City'''. * '''{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|qq=}}''' – A kofun formerly located in '''Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture'''. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a '''mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)''', are '''designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan'''.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蟹沢古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q130240468]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q130240468]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following Kofuns (kofun) in Japan:
* '''{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' – A kofun located in '''Nakano, Nagano Prefecture'''. It is a '''designated historic site of Nakano City'''. * '''{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|qq=}}''' – A kofun formerly located in '''Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture'''. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a '''mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)''', are '''designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan'''.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蟹沢古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q130240468]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q130240468]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following Kofuns (kofun) in Japan:
* '''{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' – A kofun located in '''Nakano, Nagano Prefecture'''. It is a '''designated historic site of Nakano City'''. * '''{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|qq=}}''' – A kofun formerly located in '''Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture'''. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a '''mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)''', are '''designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan'''.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蟹沢古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q130240468]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q130240468]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following Kofuns (kofun) in Japan:
* '''{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' – A kofun located in '''Nakano, Nagano Prefecture'''. It is a '''designated historic site of Nakano City'''. * '''{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|qq=}}''' – A kofun formerly located in '''Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture'''. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a '''mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)''', are '''designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan'''.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蟹沢古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蟹沢古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蟹沢古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蟹沢古墳]]
'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following Kofuns (kofun) in Japan:
* '''{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' – A kofun located in '''Nakano, Nagano Prefecture'''. It is a '''designated historic site of Nakano City'''. * '''{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|qq=}}''' – A kofun formerly located in '''Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture'''. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a '''mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)''', are '''designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan'''.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]

'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following Kofuns (kofun) in Japan:
* '''{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' – A kofun located in '''Nakano, Nagano Prefecture'''. It is a '''designated historic site of Nakano City'''. * '''{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|qq=}}''' – A kofun formerly located in '''Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture'''. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a '''mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)''', are '''designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan'''.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]

'''Kanizawa Kofun''' (蟹沢古墳) may refer to the following Kofuns (kofun) in Japan:
* '''{{ill|Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja|蟹沢古墳 (中野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanizawa Kofun (Nakano, Nagano)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' – A kofun located in '''Nakano, Nagano Prefecture'''. It is a '''designated historic site of Nakano City'''. * '''{{ill|Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun|qq=}}''' – A kofun formerly located in '''Takasaki, Gunma Prefecture'''. Artifacts excavated from the site, including a '''mirrored inscription dated "□ Shigen Year" (□始元年)''', are '''designated as National Important Cultural Properties of Japan'''.
[[zh-yue:神戸市]]
